sections.content:
The code executed 1 action in the previous sequence.

You are still on the same level.

Valid actions right now: UP, DOWN, LEFT, RIGHT, MOUSE.
Only tool: `python`. It receives `current_frame`, `previous_frame`, `history`, `transitions`, `last_transition`, `valid_actions`, `last_action_result`, and `action(actions)`. It also provides `last_animation` (per-action frames of your last `action(...)` call) and `frame_stats` (how much this game has animated so far) -- optional; use them only if you judge the intermediate motion informative rather than incidental.
Only letter-coded board views and lightweight metadata are exposed; raw numeric color IDs are not available.
Keep tool output compact: use `current_frame.segmentation` as the primary view, and `current_frame.ascii` only for a small specific region; never print full boards.
For the most recent change, compare `previous_frame` to `current_frame`, or `last_transition.before_frame` to `last_transition.after_frame`; `history[-1].frame` is the current frame, not the previous one.
Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.
Below you are provided with the current knowledge ledger from the previous turn. The default behavior is to copy it and add or remove things based on the evidence that you gathered. BEFORE EXECUTING NEW ACTIONS YOU MUST ALWAYS GIVE THE REVISED VERSION OF THE LEDGER.
Pass it as the `world_model` argument of the `python` tool call, alongside `code`. That argument is the one that carries forward, so send it on every call, even when you reply with a tool call and no assistant text.
Game-tier fields (action_semantics, object_taxonomy, hud_map, win_pattern, level_log, cross_level_notes) persist across levels; level-tier fields (world_model, goal_model, recent_findings, open_questions, current_plan, strategy_log, failed_probes) reset when a level ends. Record every unproductive probe in failed_probes so it is never repeated, and tag claims VERIFIED(step N) or HYPOTHESIS.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `run_complete`, `level_completed`, or `done`.

You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it.

Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
If you use MOUSE, include integer row and col arguments.

- Object taxonomy: {'divider_blocks': 'White 2x2 blocks at col 32-33, can be swapped with objects via UP/DOWN', 'colored_pixels': 'Small 2x2 movable objects inside containers on left panel', 'gray_containers': '4x8 gray boxes on left that hold colored pixels', 'white_containers': 'White frames on right panel containing colored shapes (red and blue)', 'blue_square': '2x4 blue block on left panel', 'red_blocks': 'Red and dark red blocks on left panel', 'pixel_pattern': 'Scattered blue/charcoal 1px objects forming a pattern on left'}
- HUD map: {'bottom_bar': 'White horizontal bar at row 63, shrinks as timer/progress indicator', 'divider': 'Vertical line of white 2x2 blocks at col 32-33, rows 10-50'}

- World model: Two-panel layout: left (charcoal bg) has colored objects in gray containers; right (black bg) has colored objects in white containers. Vertical white divider at col 32-33. Arrow keys move active object: LEFT/RIGHT within container, UP/DOWN swaps with divider blocks. Green pixel (node 34) is currently the active object, restored to original position after DOWN.
- Goal model: Move colored objects from left containers through divider to right side to match targets in white containers. Need to figure out how to select different objects.
- Recent findings: ['UP swaps active object with divider block above', 'DOWN reverses the swap', 'LEFT/RIGHT move within container bounds', 'Node IDs track objects across swaps - colors move with nodes']
- Open questions: ["How to select/activate different objects? (MOUSE doesn't seem to change selection)", 'What is the target configuration on the right side?', 'Does UP always go to the nearest divider block, or a specific one?', 'Can objects be moved from right side back to left?']

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Good - DOWN reversed the swap. Node 33 (white) is back at [[38,32],[39,33]] and node 34 (green) is back at [[40,10],[41,11]]. The board is back to the state after the first MOUSE click.
Now I need to figure out:
1. How to select different objects
2. What the goal is
Let me think about this more carefully. The green pixel (node 34) was the active object. When I pressed UP, it swapped with the divider block at approximately the same row level. But wait - the green pixel was at row 40-41, and the divider block was at row 38-39. So UP moved it to the divider block ABOVE it.
When I pressed DOWN, it swapped back. So DOWN swaps with the divider block BELOW.
Now, the key question is: how do I select a different object? Let me try clicking on a different object and then pressing arrow keys to see if a different object moves.
Let me try clicking on the yellow pixel (which is inside a gray container at the top) and then pressing arrow keys.
Actually, let me first understand the layout better. Looking at the initial segmentation:
- Node 24: yellow pixel at [[20,24],[21,25]] - inside gray container node 26
- Node 25: blue pixel at [[22,20],[23,21]] - also inside gray container node 26
- Node 26: gray container at [[20,20],[21,25],[25,25],[25,20]] - a 6x6 container
Wait, but the gray container node 26 has children [24, 25] which are the yellow and blue pixels. So both are inside the same container.
And node 32 (another gray container) has child [34] which is the green pixel.
So there are two gray containers on the left:
1. Top container (node 26): contains yellow (node 24) and blue (node 25)
2. Bottom container (node 32): contains green (node 34)
The question is: which object is "active"? It seems like the green pixel was active by default. Maybe I need to click on a different object to make it active?
Let me try clicking on the yellow pixel and then pressing RIGHT to see if the yellow pixel moves.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# DOWN reversed the swap - board is back to normal state
# Now I need to figure out how to select different objects
# Let me try clicking on the yellow pixel (row 20, col 24) and then RIGHT

# Click on yellow pixel
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 20, 'col': 24}])
print(f"After MOUSE on yellow: {last_action_result}")

# Try RIGHT to see if yellow moves
action(['RIGHT'])
print(f"After RIGHT: {last_action_result}")

seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = {n['id']: n for n in seg['nodes']}

# Check yellow pixel position
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'Y':
        print(f"Yellow: id={n['id']} boundary={n['boundary']}")
    if n['color'] == 'N':
        print(f"Green: id={n['id']} boundary={n['boundary']}")
</parameter>
<parameter=world_model>
